assertions.description: SVGUseElement is exposed; this is the element whose CSS cascade behaviour changes.

Selectors targeting the host of a <use> element apply to instantiated content, not to the originating element subtree. Set a colour via a selector that only matches the use, and verify it cascades into the cloned subtree.
assertions.test: (()=>{const ns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg';const svg=document.createElementNS(ns,'svg');svg.innerHTML='<defs><rect id="r" width="10" height="10"/></defs><g class="host"><use href="#r"/></g><style>.host { fill: rgb(0, 200, 0); }</style>';document.body.appendChild(svg);const ok=getComputedStyle(svg.querySelector('use')).fill.includes('0, 200, 0');svg.remove();return ok;})()

assertions.description: Presentation attributes on <use> still feed into the CSS cascade.
assertions.test: (()=>{const ns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg';const svg=document.createElementNS(ns,'svg');svg.innerHTML='<defs><rect id="r" width="10" height="10"/></defs><use href="#r" fill="red"/>';document.body.appendChild(svg);const ok=getComputedStyle(svg.querySelector('use')).fill.includes('255, 0, 0');svg.remove();return ok;})()

assertions.description: <use href="#id"> resolves to an element in the document.
assertions.test: (()=>{const ns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg';const svg=document.createElementNS(ns,'svg');svg.innerHTML='<defs><rect id="r2" width="10" height="10"/></defs><use href="#r2"/>';document.body.appendChild(svg);const u=svg.querySelector('use');const ok=u && u.href && (u.href.baseVal==='#r2' || u.getAttribute('href')==='#r2');svg.remove();return ok;})()
